commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=b706eb3a992581f87a68d4ff854a44654658a5dc)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an audio-only guard around Gemini CLI media autodetect, an apply-level regression test, and an audio docs note so audio falls through to provider/local transcription while image/video keep Gemini CLI.

Reproducibility: Not with a high-confidence live run in this review. Source inspection shows current main still routes audio autodetect to the generic Gemini CLI read_many_files entry, and the linked report includes concrete Telegram .ogg failure logs.
commands.review_followup: Needs human maintainer handling because the remaining blockers are contributor proof, conflict resolution, and an intentional default-routing compatibility decision rather than a safe automated repair.

target.title: fix: skip Gemini CLI audio autodetect in headless flows
target.body:
## Summary
- stop auto-selecting Gemini CLI for audio transcription in headless media-understanding flows
- keep Gemini CLI auto-detect for image/video only
- add regression coverage proving audio falls through to provider transcription even when a `gemini` binary is present

## Why
Fixes #46588.

Gemini CLI headless audio-path prompting is currently unreliable for Telegram voice notes / `.ogg` inputs: it may treat the path as plain file context instead of binary audio and drift into invalid tool fallback behavior (`run_shell_command`). Until the CLI offers a deterministic headless binary-audio path, auto-detect should prefer local/provider audio transcription backends instead of Gemini CLI for audio.

## Testing
- `corepack pnpm vitest run src/media-understanding/apply.test.ts src/media-understanding/runner.auto-audio.test.ts`
target.author: ignaciopro
